justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken with specific attack vectors (collisions, preimage, brute-force); HMAC-SHA256 explicitly recommended with correct code snippet; timing attack via === clearly identified with exploitation method; crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with Buffer conversion; timestamp design flaw thoroughly explained (token not self-contained, caller cannot recover Date.now() value); safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on MD5 — goes beyond 'broken' to cite HashClash, GPU brute-force rates, and 30-day token lifetime as compounding factors
Correctly identifies length-extension attack from naive hash(payload+secret) concatenation as a separate issue from MD5 weakness
Timestamp flaw explanation is precise — frames it as structural impossibility, not just inconvenience
Suggests JWT as standard alternative, matching the rubric's recommended fix direction
Identifies additional valid issues beyond rubric requirements: no expiration enforcement, input validation on partnerId, token migration planning
Production readiness section correctly flags that fixing requires partner migration and token re-issuance
